expectations.text: Produces a customer email with a subject line and a body of 200 words or fewer
expectations.evidence: Subject line present: 'Your follow-up still takes 30 minutes. It shouldn't.' Body word count is 123 words (well under 200 limit).

expectations.text: The email body opens on the customer's problem (e.g. unreliable action items, manual follow-ups), not on the feature announcement
expectations.evidence: Email body opens: 'You finish a meeting and sit down to write the follow-up. First you re-read the transcript. Then you sort through the action items and fix the ones that are wrong - which is nearly 1 in 3. Then you send. That's 30 minutes of work MeetFlow should be doing for you.' Grammatical subject is 'You' (customer); first sentences name the manual correction problem.

expectations.text: The email contains exactly one call to action
expectations.evidence: Single CTA found: 'Open your next meeting summary to try it.' No other imperative/action directives appear in the body.

expectations.text: Positioning grounding and a SUCCESs score for the email are still shown even though only one output was requested
expectations.evidence: Output contains a full Positioning Summary section (5 labeled bullets with citations) and a SUCCESs Scorecard table scoring the email 6/6 across all six checks.

expectations.text: Every number used appears in a repo file and is cited; no invented metrics
expectations.evidence: Numbers used: 'nearly 1 in 3' (from 34% in PRD), '30 minutes' (PRD review screen design / sarah-chen.md), 'under 2 minutes' (PRD), export channels 'email, Slack, or Notion' (PRD What It Does). All cited in Sources table. No invented numbers found.

expectations.text: No em dashes; no brand-as-hero opener like 'We're excited to announce'
expectations.evidence: Zero em dash characters found in file. Subject line and body both open on customer pain ('Your follow-up still takes 30 minutes' / 'You finish a meeting'). No brand-as-hero phrasing present.

claims.claim: Email is for Pro users and Smart Follow-Up is available now
claims.evidence: 'Smart Follow-Up is available now on your Pro plan' - consistent with PRD plan availability section and April 2026 launch (workshop now = 17-03-2026, launch is April 2026). Slight tension: 'available now' when launch is April 2026 (3 weeks away), but acceptable for a pre-launch customer email announcing availability.

suggestions.reason: No assertion checks whether the subject line itself avoids brand-as-hero framing. The current assertion only covers the body opener. A subject line like 'Introducing Smart Follow-Up' would pass all current assertions. Worth adding a subject-line check.

eval_feedback.overall: Clean 6/6. Assertions are well-chosen for this single-format eval. The one gap is the subject line not having its own hero-framing check.
